title: Stored Procedures
text: Stored Procedures Some DBMSs support a feature called a stored procedure. A stored procedure is a program written in the native query language which is compiled by the DBMS and stored in the database. Stored procedures can significantly improve performance because the DML statements have already been parsed and compiled, and can therefore be executed immediately. A common technique in DBMS connectors is to associate a standard set of stored procedures with every table. The connector may create the stored procedures when creating the table. Related Links Service Stored Procedures (SSPs) Uniface supports the execution of DBMS stored procedures by using the activate statement to address a Stored Procedure Component defined with the Signature Editor. This concept is referred to within Uniface as Service Stored Procedures. Was this topic helpful? Like Dislike
